title: Did anything interesting happen on September 13th 2000
display_title: Did anything interesting happen on September 13th 2000
wikitext:
[]: Did anything interesting happen on September 13th 2000?             Quote Post by overthehillsand » Wed Jul 01, 2020 4:02 pm Hello there. I have only recently gotten into Eva, so I searched through this forum and found that this website and many of it's users go way back. So my question to users like them, if you don't mind me asking, is did anything of note happen on the canon date of Second Impact? Was there a celebration of any kind? Did a cult form around the date and prepare for the apocalypse? Or anything in between? Thank you.

[]: Re: Did anything interesting happen on September 13th 2000?             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Jul 01, 2020 9:31 pm I very much haven't been there myself, but from some brief research and general tales I've been told, there wasn't much. I'm not ruling out the possibility that someone somewhere did something like that; if anything I'd say that as much is basically guaranteed, but it for sure didn't catch on or get any sort of bigger attention

[]: Re: Did anything interesting happen on September 13th 2000?             Quote Post by Joseki » Thu Jul 02, 2020 4:49 am https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space:_1999 In the opening episode, set in the year 1999, nuclear waste stored on the Moon's far side explodes, knocking the Moon out of orbit and sending it, as well as the 311 inhabitants of Moonbase Alpha, hurtling uncontrollably into space.

[]: Re: Did anything interesting happen on September 13th 2000?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 02, 2020 10:43 am ^ Given how much NGE’s opening titles mirrors those of UFO, another Gerry Anderson work, I wouldn’t be surprised. He probably grew up on Japanese dubs of his shows as a kid.

[]: Re: Did anything interesting happen on September 13th 2000?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 02, 2020 12:03 pm To answer the main question -- if there were any fan-events on the day, they've not left any "legendary fan event" legacy. Japanese records of the day may well have been lost, like much other material has been. Anno's Gerry Anderson fanboy is on display all over NGE, as can be seen from this picture of young Gendo and Fuyutsuki and the Space:1999 Eagle spaceship gets an outing in Ha
